heading_content_part: Privacy PolicyPrivacy Policy

All capitalized terms used in this Privacy Policy and which are not defined therein shall have the meaning ascribed to them in the NumberBook Terms of Service. Reference to “we” or “We” or “us” or “Us” in this Privacy Policy shall be deemed as references to NumberBook, and the terms “our” or “Our” will be construed accordingly. Reference to “you” or “You” in this Privacy Policy shall be deemed as references to the User as set out in the Terms of Service, and the terms “your” or “Your” will be construed accordingly.
INTRODUCTION

NumberBook’s mission is to build the most accurate and complete phone community, to provide a Smart Call ID, dialer and messaging functionality that, among other features, allow for population of unidentified numbers and other data in call display the name associated with a certain number for incoming or outgoing calls or following a manual number search against NumberBook database; For this purpose, NumberBook is depending on the Users sharing certain information. In this context, we are firmly committed to the security and protection of integrity of the Users of the Services (as defined in the Terms of Service) and their contacts.

This Privacy Policy describes what personal information You may share with NumberBook, as data controller, and other Users when activating and using the Services; and how NumberBook receives, collects and uses this data and how You may opt-out at any time, altogether preventing use of the data shared via the Services.

This Privacy Policy is incorporated into and is subject to the Terms of Service. Your use of the Service and any information You provide through the Service remains subject to this Privacy Policy and the Terms of Service.
DATA CONTROLLER AND CONTRACTING PARTY

1.1 IN BRIEF AND SIMPLE TERMS:NumberBook processes Your profile data (name, phone number etc.) and information about Your activity on the NumberBook application including device information, IP address and location for the provisioning and personalization of the Services. NumberBook has enhanced its privacy center to provide You with more ability to access and control Your data.

1.1.1Subject always to obtaining your prior consent, we may:provide your information to third parties (such as Google and advertising networks) to serve more relevant advertisements or special offers and promotions to Youprovide Your availability status to other Users (Last Seen)allow other Users, at Your option, to either obtain your contact details or send a message to You to request Your contact details1.1.2 We do not:store or share any personal information of contacts from Your address book
provide reverse number look up of contacts from Your address book1.2
INFORMATION COLLECTED AND RECEIVED

When You install the NumberBook App, NumberBook will collect some technical information from Your device in order to be able to provide You with the Services, namely Your IP address, the unique device ID number and what operating system, operator, screen resolution and version of the NumberBook App You use, if any. This information is used to verify that the User device is properly registered and will not be used to identify You personally and will not constitute personal information for the purposes of this Policy. We will, however, use this data for statistics and user support as long as You use the Services.
1.2.2 Your Profile

while You register for a user profile in the NumberBook App  the confirmation of You being the holder of the provided number is a must, Your uploaded information to the profile section of the NumberBook App will be received and stored by NumberBook until the User changes or deletes such data or terminates the Services. Compulsory information is requested when creating a profile page as the User's first name, last name and phone number; and additional optional information may be provided at Your discretion including, but not limited to, photo, gender, street address and zip code, country of residence, email address, professional website, Facebook page, Twitter address and a short status message.

Please note that the User is personally responsible for any Content uploaded to and displayed in the User profile.Upon your prior consent to Third Party's Services Privacy Policy, Third Party may supplement NumberBook with the information provided by You to the first to be added to the information provided by You e.g. demographic information and additional contact information that is publicly available.
1.2.3 Identifiers

In addition, at Your discretion, You may make available to NumberBook, and the latter will store and process, Your identifier information as Third Party services' user, i.e. Facebook, Google+, LinkedIn and Twitter, (and also a mapped photo where applicable) for application's interoperability and functionality with such Third Party services. Please note that  no passwords of any of the mentioned Third Party's services will ever be stored. Your personal information on the respective Third Party service may be subject to your control by providing us an access to through the respective Third Party service's privacy settings.

1.2.4 Your ContactsIf You decide to share your Contact Information with NumberBook, we will use Contact Information for the purpose of backing up Your contacts and helping You restoring you contact list and providing you with a smart Call ID.Sharing Contact Information with NumberBook is necessary if You wish to benefit from a smart Call ID, if you decide to share Your Contact Information, You will be able to identify Your callers cause NumberBook at a first searches your contact list, and in the event, the number was not found, NumberBook services will provide a smart lookup allowing you to identify the unknown number.

Sharing Contact Information with NumberBook is necessary if You wish to benefit from NumberBook Backup and Restore Service, especially in the case You lose Your phone or need to replace it,  You can restore Your Contact List from Your backed up one to NumberBook cloud.

Please note that the phone numbers are the sole information to  be received, stored and used from Your Contact list. Any other numbers or information (addresses, passwords and credit card numbers and any other similar information will automatically be in your device's phone book will be disregarded and will never be stored or used by NumberBook cause our safety algorithms, prevents  their collection into NumberBook database by filtering any number and information stored in the phone book in a number sequence not compatible with that used for telephone numbers.

Please also note that You always have the free choice to do share your Contacts with NumberBook and the full authority to render Your Number and all Your Contacts unavailable for search in the database by respectively, easily delisting Your Number and opting out. if You have shared information and changed Your mind, You can

4.1 NumberBook may use the personal information collected to provide You with its main functionalities and features, through processes of pertinent maintenance, improvements, analysis and customization of its Services for its Users, partners and Third Party providers and contractors. More specifically, NumberBook may use such information to:

provide smart caller ID, whether through dialer and/or messaging functionality that, among other features, allow the display of the name associated with an unidentified number for incoming or outgoing calls or the manual search for the name associated with the unidentified number against the NumberBook database;

Provide manual search for  the number associated with a certain name against the NumberBook database, in proviso, that the relevant number is available for search in a public database or relates to a User who has made his or her name available for search;On the other hand, if the number is not available for search in a public database or relates to a User who has made his or her name private, in such case Your request will be forwarded to that person via an SMS or push notification with your name and he/she may choose whether or not to share the phone number with You at his/her own discretion

implementing â€œsocial graphâ€ algorithm by displaying information based on direct or indirect connections a User may have in common with other Users and displaying â€œavailabilityâ€ functionalities of the Services;send in application push notifications and reminders and deliver messages via the Services,Preserve User spam lists and build a community-based spam blocking directory;

Interoperate with Third Party's services by enabling You to use and share Your information in connection with Your registration, login or other use of Third Party services e.g. payment service providers, social networking sites and other third party API's; andProvide customer support, respond to Your queries, investigate and resolve issues related to the Services;otherwise to enable the improvement of our Services, business and operations.

decoder_heading: 4.2 To Comply with laws and regulations and to hinder fraudulent or illegal activities
ident_heading: 4.2 To Comply with laws and regulations and to hinder fraudulent or illegal activities
heading_content_part: 4.2 To Comply with laws and regulations and to hinder fraudulent or illegal activitiesWe may use the collected information to comply with applicable laws and regulations and to enforce and implement our agreements and fulfil our obligations and to protect and defend the rights of NumberBook's Users and to verify the provided information in the User profile with third party providers and to ensure the functionality of Our Services and the  accuracy of Our data, to perform trouble-shooting and to hinder and detect fraud, any security breach or any activity  not in compliance with laws.

4.3In addition to the above, NumberBook may use the information related to the Users and Contact Information if we believe such action is necessary in order to:(a) comply with the law, or legal process served on us and to abide by clause 4.2 of this Privacy Policy;(b) protect and defend our rights or property (including the enforcement of our agreements); or(c) act in urgent circumstances to protect the personal safety of Users or members of the public.

(d) to implement our agreements and fulfil our obligations, and we insure that such agreements and statements ensure that personal information can only be used and handled for the purposes and in accordance with such agreements or statements as have been executed. Some of our agents and contractors are subject to foreign laws, including laws that may require disclosure of personal information to government agencies in those countries.

(e) We transfer information to trusted vendors, service providers, and other partners who support our business and Services, as well as potential partners who may wish to work with us to provide other services, that interact or are integrated with our Services. NumberBook will always require these third parties to take all the appropriate measures for personal information protection whether organizational and/or technical more precisely to observe the General Data Protection Regulations and any other applicable legislation.

The following is a description of how NumberBook may share information about you with trusted third party partners. Should NumberBook add additional partners, this description will be updated:NumberBook uses a third party partner company in order to send You or a third party you wish to contact SMS when you use the Services. In order to send you the SMS, we send the third party company Your and the relevant third party's phone numbers. This third party SMS partner is contractually obligated only to use these phone numbers for the transmission of requested SMS.

NumberBook may use Push Notification Services to notify you about up-dates and important information. In such cases, Your phone number may be part of the notification. This message is sent to the push notification service provider, for delivery to Your device. You may, however, at any time-opt out or disable push notifications in the NumberBook App.

NumberBook may also send a queried telephone number to Facebook in order to verify it against the Facebook API or retrieve the holders' Facebook ID or Facebook photo, if available. Connect your NumberBook account with Facebook to share posts with your Facebook friends. Sharing your NumberBook app activity helps your entourage to get to know you better and lets them discover interesting new things about you. When using the Facebook functionalities, You are bound by the Facebook Terms of Service, located at https://www.facebook.com/policies/?ref=pf, and the Facebook privacy policy, located at https://www.facebook.com/about/privacy/.

NumberBook may disclose and transfer Your Information to our associated or affiliated organizations or related entities and to any third party who acquires a right to use of our data or acquires our business, whether such acquisition is by way of merger, consolidation or purchase of all or a substantial portion of our assets.

The submission of Contact Information by You and the processing thereof by NumberBook is protected under the applicable law, see below. NumberBook may, however, disclose information contained in the Service and about you if we determine that for national security, law enforcement, or other issues of public importance that disclosure of information is necessary, always provided, of course, it is also lawful.
4.4 Cookies and Automatically Collected Information

The NumberBook Site uses so called “Cookies” in connection with the Services. Cookies are pieces of information stored on Your computer’s hard drive and/or other device’s browser that enables NumberBook to keep Your login session for thirty days. The use of Cookies is standard practice. Cookies do not enable any access to and/or inspection of other information on Your computer or other device. If You do not wish to receive cookies, or wish to be notified of when they are placed, You may set your web browser to do so, if your browser so permits. Please understand that if Cookies are turned off, You may not be able to obtain full functionality in certain parts of the Services.

The NumberBook Site and App use Google Analytics, a web analytics solution by Google. Google Analytics also adds cookies to Your computer when You visit our website or interact with certain parts of the NumberBook App and collects usage information to create statistics for NumberBook. See Google’s privacy policy for details.

The NumberBook Site also embeds badges from the social networking sites Facebook, Google¬+ and Twitter. The badges may add cookies to Your computer or mobile device when You use the Services and have enabled Facebook connection. See Facebook’s privacy policy, Google’s privacy policy and Twitter’s privacy policy for details.

NumberBook servers may automatically collect data about Your Internet address when You use the Services. This information, known as an Internet Protocol address, or “IP Address”, is a number that is automatically assigned to Your computer or other device by Your Internet service provider whenever You are on the Internet. When You request pages from the Services, our servers may log Your IP Address and, if applicable, Your domain name. Your IP Address is used to help identify You and to gather demographic information about our Users as a whole. NumberBook servers may also record the referring page that linked you to NumberBook (e.g. another web site or a search engine); the pages You visit on the Services; the web site you visit after NumberBook Site; the ads you see and/or click on; other information about the type of Web browser, computer, platform, related software and settings you are using; any search terms You have entered on this web site or a referral site; and other Web usage activity and data logged by NumberBook’s servers. NumberBook uses this information for internal system administration, to help diagnose problems with servers, and to administer our Services. Such information may also be used to gather demographic information, such as country of origin and Internet Service Provider.
Corresponding data may be collected and used for the same purposes when You enable and use the “search the web” functionality within the NumberBook App.

We take all reasonable precautions to protect personal information from misuse, loss and unauthorized access. NumberBook assures You that it has implemented physical, electronic, and procedural safeguards in order to protect the information, including that the information will be stored on secured servers and protected by secured networks to which access is limited to a few authorized employees and personnel. However, no method of transmission over the Internet or method of electronic storage is 100% secure.
LEGAL BASES AND DATA RETENTION

NumberBook will collect and process Your personal information in order to perform the contract with You (e.g. to deliver the Services), based on Your consent (i.e. the choices You make upon registration and in Your settings) or based on a legitimate interest of NumberBook (which is not overridden by your interests or fundamental rights and freedoms). Such legitimate interests of NumberBook include to protect and maintain the security of the Services, to create and maintain spam blocking directories, to understand and improve our Services and user relationships and to administer our business and customer support. Where we process data based on Your consent,
You may withdraw your consent at any time via Your settings.

NumberBook will retain Your personal information while You maintain a user profile in the Services. You may at any time choose to delete Your user profile and Your personal information via Your settings. If You make such choices, Your personal information will no longer be visible to others in the Services. We will retain Your personal information for the period necessary to fulfill the purposes outlined in this Privacy Policy unless a longer retention period is required or permitted by law. We may also retain Your personal information in order to accommodate Your choices regarding marketing.

CHILDREN’S PRIVACYWe do not intentionally or knowingly collect or solicit information from children under the relevant age of consent under the laws of their respective jurisdictions or knowingly allow such persons to register for the Services. The Services are not directed to children under the relevant age of consent under the laws of their respective jurisdictions. If you believe that we might have information from or about a child under the relevant age of consent, please contact us at
support@numberbook.net.
Amendments to this Privacy Policy

NumberBook may at any time, follow the notification of the User by push notification or by publication on NumberBook Site or by any other means, amend the term of this Privacy Policy, and You are encouraged to review this Policy from time to time. If You object to any changes, You may uninstall NumberBook application and cease using the Services. Your continued use of the Services after NumberBook has communicated the changes and amendments to this Privacy Policy, will mean that the collection, use, sharing and processing of Your personal information is subject to the new and updated Privacy Policy. We may from time to time set out more detailed information on how we process your personal information in relation to particular Services in supplemental Privacy Notices which shall be incorporated into and form part of this Privacy Policy.
